description: Load-bearing evidence a strong report should surface. Grader-side artifact; not bundled into the case. Every item ties to one or more rubric axes so we can measure coverage after the fact and catch axes that lack grounding.

load_bearing_evidence.description: The [N, chunk] fp32 intermediate materialised per chunk inside _ChunkedLogProbEntropyFn (logits_f, chunk_exp, p, grad_logits). Chunk 4x -> slab 4x; this is the per-rank memory cost of raising chunk_size.
A warnings.warn(below 8192) validator is the only artifact PR #1892 shipped to defend the stability claim. No ablation, no loss-divergence plot, no unit test.
SFT config forces fused_lm_head_chunk_size = 'disabled' via validator, bypassing the knob entirely. RL-vs-SFT structural asymmetry.
benchmarks/scripts/run_single_benchmark.py never passes --model.fused-lm-head-chunk-size, so every RL benchmark takes the default. Explains why the regression was inevitable across the fleet.
In the failing CI run at PR #1892, per-step Peak Memory jumps at Step 1 (25.9 -> 31.8 GiB) then holds stable through Step 4. Tells you it's allocation on first training step, not a leak.
test_benchmark_regression.py uses 1% MEMORY_TOLERANCE vs 10% METRIC_TOLERANCE on MFU/throughput/step_time. Why only memory breached, not perf metrics.
PR #1525 introduced fused_lm_head_chunk_size with default None. First of three under-justified changes to this parameter.
PR #1649 refactored to int | Literal['auto', 'disabled'] with auto=2048. Second change; also shipped without ablation.

The 60 baselines span 5 model families (Qwen3-0.6B, Qwen3-4B, Qwen3-30B, Qwen3-235B, INTELLECT-3). Vocab ~151k for Qwen3 family. Memory cost of the slab scales linearly with vocab, so larger-vocab families are more exposed.
locations: tokenizer_config.json files of each model family (not bundled — agent must infer from model names)

load_bearing_evidence.description: 60 baseline JSONs in benchmarks/baselines/ but only 2 of them (1xa6000 and 4xa6000 rl-full flash_attention_2 seq=65k) are asserted in test_benchmark_regression.py. Structural hole — 58 configs can silently drift.

coverage_thresholds.4: report substantively engages with >=9 of the 13 items
